original: Ötzi the Iceman, a five-thousand-year-old mummy, is discovered by German tourists in the Italian Alps, the oldest known natural human mummy in Europe

Japanese 2-way phenomShohei Ohtanibecomes first player in MLB history to hit 50 home runs and steal 50 bases in a season; slugs 3 HRs in a 10 RBI game as the LA Dodgers rout the Miami Marlins 20-4 in Miami

US women's all-round gymnastics team is controversially penalised 0.5 after compulsory round at the Seoul Olympics; substitute an alternate athlete on uneven bars medal podium; Americans finish 4th just 0.3 behind East Germany
Jim Abbott, American MLB baseball pitcher born with only one hand, 1989-99 (Olympic gold 1988; California Angels, NY Yankees, and 2 other teams), born in Flint, Michigan
New Zealand becomes the first country to grant all women the right to vote
Hyperion, moon of Saturn - the first non-round moon - discovered by William Cranch Bond, his son George Phillips Bond and William Lassell
Scientists discover site of the Amazon's tallest tree, an angelim vermelho at 88.5 meters (290 feet) tall and 9.9 meters (32 feet) wide, Iratapuru River Nature Reserve, northern Brazil,
Sunita Williams, American NASA astronaut and naval officer (set record most spacewalks by a woman), born in Euclid, Ohio
Japan beats South Africa 34-32 in Brighton, England in the biggest upset in Rugby World Cup history
Cubs sluggerErnie Bankshits record 5th grand slam of season
Betty and Barney Hill claim that they saw a mysterious craft in the sky and that it tried to abduct them.
Cumbre Vieja volcano erupts on the Spanish island of La Palma, its first eruption since 1971
North America has lost 3 billion birds (29%) since 1970 according to analysis published in "Science"
British monarchQueen Elizabeth IIinterred at Windsor Castle after a state funeral at Westminster Abbey in London with an unprecedented 500 world leaders in attendance
Arthur Rackham, English book illustrator (Grimm's Fairy Tales, Rip van Winkle), born in London, England (d. 1939)
3-D short film "Captain EO" withMichael Jacksonpremieres at Disney theme parks
"Adventures of Superman" TV series starringGeorge Reevespremieres in syndication in the US
"Doogie Howser, M.D.", starringNeil Patrick Harrisas a teenage physician, debuts on ABC
Adam West [William West Anderson], American actor (Batman, Last Precinct), born in Walla Walla, Washington (d. 2017)
Chubby Checker's cover version of "The Twist" hits #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
"LIFE" magazine celebrates artist Anna Mary "Grandma" Moses' 100th birthday by putting her on their cover
Aleksandr Karelin, Russian Greco-Roman wrestler who went undefeated for 13 years and won 3 Olympic golds (1988; 1992; 1996), born in Novosibirsk, Russia
Ken Griffey Jr.pinch-hits his 400th home run becoming the first major league player to reach the mark as a pinch-hitter
Jacques Lusseyran, Frenchauthorand resistance leader who was blind from age 7, born in Paris, France (d. 1971)
Nile Rodgers, American guitarist (Chic - "Le Freak"), and music producer (Sister Sledge; David Bowie - "Let's Dance"), born in The Bronx, New York, City
"Fawlty Towers" starringJohn Cleese, Prunella Scales and Andrew Sachs premieres on BBC2 in the UK
Jackie Robinsonis named 1947 "Rookie of the Year"
Sergei Boebka pole vaults world record (6.13m)
Australian swimmer Duncan Armstrong sets world record 1:47.25 to win the 200m freestyle gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; beats Anders Holmertz of Sweden by 0.64
British swimmer Adrian Moorehouse wins 100m breaststroke gold medal in 1:02.04 at the Seoul Olympics; edges Károly Güttler of Hungary by 0.01
Volcanic eruption destroys the town of Rabaul on New Britain, Papua New Guinea
The Blackpool Illuminations are switched on for the first time.
First carpet sweeper patented by Melville Bissell of Grand Rapids, Michigan
New York concert promoter Sid Bernstein offers $230 million to charity for a Beatles reunion; they politely decline
Simon & Garfunkel reunite for a concert in Central Park, NYC,
NFL Oakland Raider wide receiverJerry Riceends NFL streak of 274 consecutive games with a reception in a 13-10 victory over Buffalo Bills
San Diego Chargers' kicker John Carney boots 6 field goals in an 18-17 win over Houston to set a new NFL record with 29 consecutive field goals made
Thierry Henryscores his first goal for Barcelona in a 3–0 Champions League group stage win over Lyon
Columbia Records releases "Cheek To Cheek", a duet album of jazz standards by singersTony BennettandLady Gaga; debuts at #1 on Billboard 200, Jazz Albums, and Traditional Jazz Albums charts.
"Janet Jackson's Rhythm Nation 1814" 4th studio album by Janet Jackson is released (Grammy Award Best Long Form Music Video 1990, Billboard Album of the Year 1990)
Cheri Oteri, American comedian and actress (Scary Movie, Saturday Night Live), born in Upper Darby, Pennsylvania
Jarvis Cocker, British pop singer-songwriter (Pulp - "Common People"), and radio personality, born in Sheffield, England
Katrina McClain, American basketball forward (Olympic gold 1996), born in Charleston, South Carolina
Richard M. Linnehan, American astronaut (STS 78, sk: 90), born in Lowell, Massachusetts
Marshall Jefferson, American musician 'father of house music' (Move your Body), born in Chicago, Illinois
"Ordinary People" film directed by Robert Redford and starring Donald Sutherland,Mary Tyler Mooreand Judd Hirsch premieres (Academy Award - Best Picture, 1981)
Britain & China complete a proposed agreement to transfer Hong Kong to China by 1997
Magnitude 7.6 earthquake strikes western Mexico on exact anniversary of two previous temblors in 1985 and 2017, killing at least two people,
Nigerian authorities says over 300 people have died and 100,000 have been displaced by the worse floods in many years
Tropical Depression Imelda hits southeast Texas and Louisiana prompting widespread evacuations
Korean joint summit in Pyongyang agrees to limit North Korea's weapons programme and to South Korea economic cooperation
English forces underEdward the Black Princedefeat the French at the Battle of Poitiers and capture French King John II during the Hundred Years' War
George Washington's farewell address as US President is published
Rebels under Nathaniel Bacon set Jamestown, Virginia, on fire
Battle of Freeman's Farm (Bemis Heights) or 1st Battle of Saratoga
The wattle is declared Australia's national flower and incorporated into the Commonwealth Coat of Arms

Demolition begins of JFK Stadium in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; built in 1926 as Sesquicentennial Stadium, also known as Municipal Stadium, it hosted the 1926 Tunney-Dempsey fight, 41 Army-Navy football games, The Beatles; The Rolling Stones; victory rallies for The NHL Flyers and MLB Phillies; and the US portion of Live-Aid
St Kitts & Nevis declares independence from the United Kingdom
Chase Manhattan Discovery Center at Brooklyn Botanic Garden opens
Satellites China 10 & 11 launched into Earth orbit by B-1 rocket
